facts.text: Miami was named after the Miami River, derived from Mayaimi, the historic name of Lake Okeechobee and the Native Americans who lived around it.
Julia Tuttle, dubbed “the mother of Miami,” convinced Henry Flagler to extend the Florida East Coast Railway, sparking the city’s founding.
PortMiami holds the title of the world's busiest cruise port, handling more than 2.5 million passengers annually.
The Rickenbacker Causeway, the southernmost causeway, links Brickell to Virginia Key and Key Biscayne, offering scenic ocean views.
Miami’s five major sports teams span every major league: MLS, MLB, NHL, NBA, and NFL, reflecting the city’s diverse athletic culture.
The Vizcaya Museum and Gardens, a National Historic Landmark, sits on a 28‑acre estate in Coconut Grove, showcasing early 20th‑century architecture.
